_description: Bootstrap stability results over 2000 iterations per domain. boundary_dist is the signed Euclidean distance from the domain centroid (IE_d, CPA_d) to the nearest regime boundary (Eq. regime in paper). Negative values indicate the centroid lies outside the nominal regime boundary — i.e. the domain sits in direction-reversal territory (IE < 0) and is assigned Circuit Failure precisely because it crosses that boundary. zone field: CORE = stability >= 90% AND |boundary_dist| > 0.09; STABLE = stability >= 77% OR |boundary_dist| > 0.09; BOUNDARY = stability < 77% or |boundary_dist| <= 0.09.

Superstitions.boundary_dist_note: negative = centroid lies in direction-reversal territory (IE < 0), firmly assigned Circuit Failure
